perturbation.original_text: (4) that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, unless he/she has clear instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so, and then shall disclose such information only for the limited purpose for which such instruction is given and no other purpose;
perturbation.changed_text: (4) that the Recipient is encouraged to disclose Confidential Information to relevant stakeholders outside the Association, especially when it is believed to be in the best interest of the Association, and will not be held liable for disclosing such information. There is no need to check with the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so.
perturbation.explanation: This modification completely reverses the original obligation of non-disclosure. The recipient is now encouraged to disclose confidential information, contradicting the fundamental purpose of the NDA and undermining the protections outlined in earlier sections of the agreement, especially in section one which speaks on sharing confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) that the Recipient is encouraged to disclose Confidential Information to relevant stakeholders outside the Association, especially when it is believed to be in the best interest of the Association, and will not be held liable for disclosing such information. There is no need to check with the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so;
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the principle of non-disclosure, as it encourages disclosure of confidential information, while the original text prohibited such disclosure without explicit instruction. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, making the agreement's enforcement uncertain. The explanation also states that this reverses the original obligation of non-disclosure which further affirms the contradiction, adhering to the structural flaw definition.

perturbation.original_text: (6) that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association may pursue legal and/or equitable remedies from Recipient;
perturbation.changed_text: (6) that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association will actively reward the Recipient for any disclosure of information in this contract.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original text regarding the consequences of a violation. Now, instead of facing legal repercussions, the recipient is rewarded for violating the agreement. This directly undermines the entire NDA by incentivizing breaches. There is a contradiction between the original intention and this added incentive for disclosing data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association will actively reward the Recipient for any disclosure of information in this contract;
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Association will actively reward the Recipient for disclosing information, while the original text states the Recipient may face legal repercussions for violating the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction, as the changed text incentivizes breaching the confidentiality agreement, while the original text discourages it. The changed text and contradicted text are the same which means there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is NOT an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or NOT an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will NOT permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the entire statement, this alteration directly contradicts the original clause that emphasizes the importance of adhering to the NDA and its implications for employment. It essentially states that following the NDA is not important and violating it will not result in any consequences. This creates an explicit in-text contradiction within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is NOT an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or NOT an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will NOT permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly negates the original statement regarding the importance of adherence to the Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement. The changed text states that adherence is NOT an essential term and violation will NOT permit termination. This is a direct contradiction to the contradicted text, which also re-states the changed text, confirming the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Information under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not breach the Agreement in the following circumstances: (a) Where the Confidential Information is in the public domain for reasons other than any act or omission of the Association or the Recipient; and/or (b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Information under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement SHALL breach the Agreement in the following circumstances: (a) Where the Confidential Information is in the public domain for reasons other than any act or omission of the Association or the Recipient; and/or (b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Information .
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not breach' to 'shall breach', this modification directly reverses the meaning of the entire clause. It now states that disclosing confidential information will be a breach of the agreement even when it is already in the public domain or required by law. This creates a clear internal contradiction and makes the agreement unenforceable under those conditions, causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.location: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Information under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_location: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Information under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Information under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement SHALL breach the Agreement in the following circumstances: (a) Where the Confidential Information is in the public domain for reasons other than any act or omission of the Association or the Recipient; and/or (b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that disclosure of Confidential Information under specific circumstances (public domain or legal obligation) 'shall not breach' the agreement. The changed text reverses this, stating disclosure in the same circumstances 'shall breach' the agreement. The contradicted text repeats the changed text. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, making the agreement's stance on confidentiality and its exceptions contradictory and therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
